overview:
**Very High fire danger continues today, driven by critically dry fuels — ERC at 91% and 1000-hour fuels at 11%, both above local thresholds.**

- Danger stays Very High through Monday before easing to High Tuesday-Thursday as rain chances build.
- Hot, dry pattern persists with afternoon storm chances (40% today) but little meaningful rain until midweek.
- 2 of 7 local watchout thresholds are currently met (ERC and 1000-hr fuels); no Red Flag Warning is active today, though a Hydrologic Outlook is posted for the area.
- Montrose and Ouray counties remain under Stage 2 fire restrictions, with BLM lands in Gunnison, Hinsdale, Montrose, Ouray, Saguache, and San Juan counties under Stage 1 restrictions, and the Tres Rios and Gunnison Field Offices under Stage 2.
- The Gold Mountain Fire, burning in southwest Colorado, originating just two miles north of the City of Ouray, has burned 37,734 acres and remains 13% contained, with closures and evacuations still active in Ouray and Gunnison counties.
lead: Very High fire danger continues today, driven by critically dry fuels — ERC at 91% and 1000-hour fuels at 11%, both above local thresholds.

sources.name: Southwest District Fire Restrictions | Bureau of Land Management
